metadata.name: Modal Choice — Abzan Charm modes 1 and 0
metadata.description: Tests the modal choice system (CR 700.2) using Abzan Charm ({W}{B}{G}). Scenario 1: p1 casts Abzan Charm choosing mode 1 (draw 2 cards, lose 2 life). Both players pass priority. The spell resolves — p1 draws 2 cards and loses 2 life, leaving p1 at 38 life with a hand of 2 cards. Scenario 2: p2 controls a Bladetusk Boar (3/2, power >= 3). P1 casts a second Abzan Charm choosing mode 0 (exile target creature with power 3 or greater). Both players pass priority. The spell resolves — Bladetusk Boar is exiled. Both scenarios exercise CR 700.2 (modal spells, choose one) and CR 700.2a (must choose exactly one mode at cast time).
metadata.generation_notes: Abzan Charm (abzan_charm.rs) has ModeSelection with min_modes=1 max_modes=1 and three modes: mode 0=ExileObject targeting DeclaredTarget{0} (creature with min_power 3), mode 1=Sequence[DrawCards{count:2,player:Controller}, LoseLife{amount:2,player:Controller}], mode 2=AddCounter{2x +1/+1 on DeclaredTarget{1}}. The card definition declares two shared targets: index 0 = TargetCreatureWithFilter{min_power:3}, index 1 = TargetCreatureWithFilter{controller:You}. Even when choosing mode 1 (no targets used), both targets are declared in the action to match the flat shared target list pattern (same as Blessed Alliance script 148 which always declares all targets). For mode 1 (scenario 1): p1 needs 2 cards in library for the draw; p1 starts with empty hand so after drawing 2, hand count is 2; p1 goes from 40 to 38 life. For mode 0 (scenario 2): p2 controls Bladetusk Boar (3/2) which satisfies min_power >= 3; after resolution Bladetusk Boar is in exile. cast_spell_modal action passes modes=[1] or modes=[0] per CR 700.2a. Mana cost {W}{B}{G} = white:1, black:1, green:1 for both casts. Two Abzan Charm copies in p1 hand. P1 also has a Llanowar Elves on battlefield as target index 1 (TargetCreatureWithFilter{controller:You}) for both casts, satisfying the flat target requirement even when mode 2 is not chosen.

script.step_note: Scenario 1: p1 casts Abzan Charm choosing mode 1 — draw 2 cards and lose 2 life

actions.description: Initial: p1 at 40 life, 2x Abzan Charm in hand, stack empty

actions.note: CR 700.2a: p1 chooses mode 1 (draw 2, lose 2 life). Both flat targets declared per card definition. Cost {W}{B}{G} paid.

actions.description: After casting: Abzan Charm on stack, one copy remains in hand
actions.note: Abzan Charm moved from hand to stack on cast.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — stack resolves top object.

resolution.note: CR 608.2: Mode 1 resolves — p1 draws 2 cards.
CR 118.2: Mode 1 continues — p1 loses 2 life (40 → 38).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Abzan Charm resolves with mode 1 selected. Sequence: DrawCards then LoseLife.

actions.description: After mode 1 resolution: p1 at 38 life, hand count 3 (1 remaining + 2 drawn), stack empty
actions.note: p1 drew 2 cards from library (hand: 1 remaining Charm + 2 drawn = 3) and lost 2 life (40→38). Abzan Charm is in p1 graveyard.

script.step_note: Scenario 2: p1 casts second Abzan Charm choosing mode 0 — exile target creature with power 3 or greater

actions.note: CR 700.2a: p1 chooses mode 0 (exile target creature with power >= 3). Target is p2's Bladetusk Boar (3/2). Cost {W}{B}{G} paid.

actions.description: After casting: second Abzan Charm on stack, hand contains only drawn cards
actions.note: Second Abzan Charm is now on the stack targeting p2's Bladetusk Boar.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — stack resolves top object.

resolution.note: CR 701.10a: Mode 0 resolves — Bladetusk Boar (3/2, power >= 3) is exiled.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Abzan Charm resolves with mode 0 selected. Bladetusk Boar moves to exile zone.

actions.description: After mode 0 resolution: Bladetusk Boar in exile, p2 battlefield empty, stack empty
actions.note: Bladetusk Boar was exiled by Abzan Charm mode 0. p2 has no creatures on battlefield. p1 life remains 38.
